Phoenix is the sixth largest city in the United States with 1.6 million in the city and 4 million in the metro region.  Whether you're looking to soak up the sun, explore the outdoors, or immerse yourself in culture, Phoenix has something for everyone. Phoenix, the capital of Arizona, offers a diverse and unconventional leisure experience. With a variety of interesting museums, scenic parks, first-class golf courses, historical sites, and stunning natural areas, boredom is out of the question. Downtown Phoenix is a must-visit for its vibrant atmosphere, filled with restaurants, bars, galleries, and cultural venues. The Desert Botanical Garden showcases a unique collection of plants from different world deserts, while the Phoenix Zoo provides a family-friendly encounter with over 3,000 fauna members. The Arizona Science Center offers interactive exhibits that make science entertaining for all ages. Paradise Valley, a suburban neighborhood, attracts visitors with its natural attractions and cozy atmosphere. Papago Park, just a short drive from the city, offers scenic hiking trails, outlandish natural beauty, and panoramic views. Camelback Mountain and South Mountain Park provide breathtaking hiking experiences and picturesque vistas. For a change of pace, head to Old Town Scottsdale, known for its restaurants, bars, nightclubs, and art installations. Taliesin West, a natural historic landmark, offers tours of the famous Sonoran Desert home. Goldfield Ghost Town allows you to travel back in time and experience the Wild West. With stunning scenery, active entertainment, great weather, rich culture, and photogenic locations, Phoenix is a city that should be on your travel list. Let us know in the comments which city you would like to see next! Explore the world, make memories, and don't forget to like, subscribe, and stay tuned for more exciting videos from Scantrip!

videos.transcript: If you're thinking about moving to Phoenix, then this is the video for you. Today, we are breaking down the top five places to live here in the Phoenix Valley. And you might be surprised at some of the cities that actually make this list. If you've been researching Phoenix, you know that it is a huge city. It's actually a top five city in terms of population, but what makes it unique is how spread out the Phoenix Valley is. Both Mesa and Surprise are suburbs of Phoenix, but they are over an hour apart from each other. So narrowing down the different places to live can be difficult to say the least. Hopefully today I will be able to help with that by talking about the top five best ranked places to live in the Phoenix Valley. To rank these, I am using my experience as a born and raised Phoenix native, as well as many online ranking and city reviews to verify that others feel the same way that I do. My name is Cody. I'm a local Phoenix realtor, and I specialize in helping people make the move to Arizona from other states. So, if you're thinking about making the move, you're in the right place. Kicking things off at number five on our list, we have Peoria. Peoria is located in the Northwest Valley and is really known for being a family-friendly area with good schools and low crime, specifically in the North Poria location. North Peoria is usually defined as being north of Union Hills Road within the city of Peoria. I specify North Peoria because this area is going to be newer and just overall more desirable than the southern portion of the city where crime can be a bit higher. This northern portion of the city is characterized by beautiful mountain views, clean streets, and a wide array of neighborhood options to fit different lifestyles. If you want a master plan neighborhood with palm treelined streets where no blade of grass is out of place, they have it. If you prefer to have no HOA and a larger backyard with space to store your RV, your boat, or your ATV, they have that, too. North Poria is honestly becoming one of the most affluent areas in all of Phoenix. With high-paying jobs coming to the area like the TSMC microchip plant and the desirable schooling system and outdoor lifestyle attracting many families here, this city will offer an easy escape to the outdoors with a plethora of hiking along with quick access to Phoenix's largest lake, Lake Pleasant. Here you have a huge marina where you can rent a boat and go tubing, eat at the lakeside restaurant, or just enjoy some fishing. Along with the easy access to the lake, you can also get to northern Arizona very quickly. You can be in the snow in the winter within about 1 and 1/2 hours or to Sedona to go hiking in just over an hour. The population of Peoria sits just under 200,000 residents. So, it's not a small city, but it's so large and spread out that it does not feel overcrowded. You can find many home options here, from brand new homes in North Peoria to houses built in the 1980s in South Peoria. The median home sales price is about $543,000, but you can find options quite a bit lower than that. Or if you're looking for a more luxurious home, then you can easily push to or over $1 million. As far as entertainment, Peoria is not known for its world-class entertainment or dining.
